chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُتَمَتِّعِ يَنْسَى أَنْ يُقَصِّرَ حَتَّى يُهِلَّ بِالْحَجِّ أَوْ يَحْلِقَ رَأْسَهُ أَوْ يَقَعَ أَهْلَهُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُقَصِّرَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 147 – The performer of Tumatto forgets to shorten (hair, nails etc.) until he begins with the Hajj, or he shaves off his head, or he falls upon his wife before he shortens (hair, nails, etc.)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/84)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/84)
text: يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُتَمَتِّعِ بِالْعُمْرَةِ إِلَى الْحَجِّ إِذَا أَحَلَّ أَنْ لَا يَلْبَسَ قَمِيصاً وَ لْيَتَشَبَّهْ بِالْمُحْرِمِينَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hafs Bin Al Bakhtary, from someone else,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘It is befitting for the performer of Tumatto with the Umra to the Hajj, when he is free from Ihraam, that he should not wear a shirt, and let him resemble himself with the ones in Ihraam’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtariy from more than one person who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is very proper for one who is for al-Hajj al-Tamattu‘ (advantageous), that after coming out of the state of Ihram, he should not dress up with a shirt to remain like those in the state of Ihram.’”
